params.text:
Rescue breaths and using an AED

Rescue breaths (30:2)

If you are trained and willing, combine compressions with rescue breaths in a ratio of 30 compressions to 2 breaths. After 30 compressions, open the airway again with head-tilt chin-lift, pinch the nose, seal your lips around their mouth and blow steadily until the chest just begins to rise. Give two breaths, then return to compressions.

Compression-only CPR: if you are untrained, unwilling, or unable to give rescue breaths, do continuous chest compressions without stopping. This is far better than doing nothing.

Using an AED

  • Turn the AED on as soon as it arrives and follow the spoken voice prompts.
  • Attach the pads to the person's bare, dry chest exactly as shown in the pictures on the pads.
  • Stand clear and make sure nobody touches the person while it analyses the heart rhythm.
  • If a shock is advised, make sure everyone is clear, then press the shock button. Do not touch the person during analysis or shock.
  • Immediately restart compressions after the shock and keep following the AED's prompts.

params.question: An AED has arrived and is analysing the heart rhythm. What should everyone do?

answers.text: Keep doing chest compressions to be safe

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. Movement interferes with the AED's analysis. Everyone must stand clear while it analyses.

answers.text: Make sure nobody is touching the person while it analyses

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Touching the person can disrupt the analysis, and you must also be clear before any shock is delivered.

answers.text: Turn the AED off and restart it

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. Leave the AED on and simply follow its voice prompts.

answers.text: Remove the pads and reposition them elsewhere

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. Leave the pads in the positions shown and let the AED analyse and advise.
